home.question: What kinds of projects do you take on?
home.answer: Websites, mobile apps, custom systems, AI features, marketing, branding, product photography, even student projects and grant-funded builds. If it is digital, we probably do it.

home.question: I need something that does not fit neatly into one service, what do I do?
home.answer: Tell us the outcome you want. Most real projects mix two or three services, like a website plus ads, or a system plus a rebrand, and we scope the right combination.

home.question: How is pricing structured?
home.answer: Most engagements are quote-based after a scoping call, since scope drives cost more than a flat package ever could.

home.question: Who will I actually be working with?
home.answer: You work directly with the engineer building your project, not an account manager relaying messages.

contact.question: How fast do you respond?
contact.answer: We aim to respond to every enquiry within one business day.

contact.question: What should I include in my message?
contact.answer: A short description of what you need, your rough timeline, and a budget range, since it helps us scope an honest answer faster.

pricing.question: Are these prices fixed, or do they change after we start?
pricing.answer: Every quote is fixed after scoping. We agree on deliverables and price upfront, so there are no surprise line items once the project is underway.

pricing.question: Do I need to pay everything upfront?
pricing.answer: No. Projects are billed in milestones, typically a deposit to start, then progress payments tied to delivery stages.

pricing.question: What if my project doesn't fit neatly into one tier?
pricing.answer: Most real projects blend tiers, like a Launch website with one Build feature. Tell us the outcome you want and we'll scope the right mix.

pricing.question: How long does a typical project take?
pricing.answer: Launch sites usually run 2 to 4 weeks, Build projects 6 to 10 weeks, and Enterprise systems are scoped individually based on complexity.
